title: tephrosia oxygona subsp. lactea
intro: Tephrosia oxygona subsp. lactea, also known as white milk bean, is a plant species within the pea family Fabaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, fever, and respiratory issues.
